title: Simple direct PO end-to-end
context: You're a buyer who knows the supplier code and just needs a quick PO. Test that the bot picks the right journey, extracts all slots from natural language, and fires POCRTMAINSBT (not the combined POCRTMAINTRN4).
turns: I need to create a direct purchase order.
Supplier is SUP-100, numbering series PO/STD/2026, today, USD, buyer BUY-77.
Add one line: ITM-200, 10 units at 50 USD each, need date 30 days from now.
Just create it, don't approve yet.
check: Journey locked = PoCrt at turn 1
All slots extracted with correct values (supplier_code=SUP-100, currency=USD, etc.)
Final TRANS = POCRTMAINSBT (NOT POCRTMAINTRN4 — user said 'don't approve')
SP chain shows pocrmn_sp_crt_hdrsav → hdrchk → crtgrd → init_suptxreg → aprhref → crtgrd_out
Session memory mints a po_number (PO-NEW-001) after the commit

title: Create from quotation (two-screen flow)
context: Test that the bot recognizes 'I have a quotation' as PoCrtQtn (not PoCrt), fires the entry-screen search first, then the main commit.
turns: I have an approved quotation, QTN-2024-7821, that I want to turn into a PO.
Yes, use all the lines from it as-is.
Create the PO.
check: Journey locked = PoCrtQtn (NOT PoCrt)
Entry-search POCRTQTNTRN1 fires (look for the read chip in trace)
Final commit = POCRTQTNSUB
quotation_no slot = QTN-2024-7821

title: Capital PO with proposal (data-triggered splice)
context: Test the bot's splice detection: 'Capital PO' should trigger po_type==Capital splice, which requires capital_proposal_id as additional slot.
turns: Create a Capital PO for supplier SUP-CAP-1 — buying a new milling machine.
The capital proposal is CAP-PROP-2026-04.
Buyer is BUY-77, numbering PO/CAP/2026, USD, today. One line: ITM-MILL-X, 1 unit at 75000.
Create it for approval.
check: splice_triggered = po_type==Capital (appears in trace from turn 1)
additional_required_slots includes capital_proposal_id
Bot picks POCRTMAINSBT (not TRN4) — 'for approval' means submit + someone else approves later
capital_proposal_id slot = CAP-PROP-2026-04

title: Cross-journey: create → amend (session memory)
context: Critical test for session memory. After bot creates a PO and mints a po_number, when the user says 'now amend it' the bot should switch journeys AND carry the synthetic po_number forward — no need to provide it explicitly.
turns: Create and approve a PO for SUP-300, USD, today, BUY-5, ITM-12 100 units at 5. I have approval rights.
Wait, I need to bump that quantity to 150.
Amend and approve it in one shot.
check: Turn 1: journey=PoCrt, fires POCRTMAINTRN4 (user explicitly asked for create AND approve)
Session memory shows PO-NEW-001 produced
Turn 2: journey switches to PoAmnd
Turn 3: fires POAMDENTTRN1 (entry-search) and POAMDMAINTRN5 (amend+approve)
po_number slot is auto-filled from session memory (PO-NEW-001) — user never typed it

title: Approve an existing PO
context: Test that the bot recognizes 'approve PO-X' as PoApp, fires entry-search first to load, then the approve commit.
turns: I need to approve PO-2026-410.
Yes, show me the details before I approve.
All looks fine, approve it.
check: Journey locked = PoApp at turn 1
po_number slot = PO-2026-410
Entry-search POAPPENTTRN1 fires
Final commit = POAPPMAINSBT (NOT POAPPMAINTRN4 — TRN4 in PoApp means 'Get Quote Line No', a completely different task)

title: Lookup-only: view PO status
context: Test that the bot recognizes a question as lookup (not a commit). Should fire ONLY POVWENTTRN1, no main-screen TRANS.
turns: What's the status of PO-2026-820?
When was it approved and by whom?
check: Journey locked = PoViw
TRANS fired = POVWENTTRN1 (a READ, marked as 'read' kind in trace)
NO write-kind TRANS fires (PoViw is read-only)
Bot's response asks no clarification — it has the po_number

title: Hold a PO with reason (free-text slot)
context: Test free-text slot extraction. The reason for the hold is conversational; bot should capture it cleanly.
turns: Put PO-2026-702 on hold — there's a quality issue we need to investigate.
Confirm the hold.
check: Journey locked = PoHold
Entry-search POHLDENTTRN1 fires (load the PO)
Final commit = POHLDMAINSBT (Change Status)
hold_reason slot is captured with the quality-issue phrasing

title: Short-close after partial delivery
context: Test that bot recognizes a partial-delivery scenario. Multiple slots in one sentence (PO number, line, quantities, reason).
turns: On PO-2026-805, line 3 we ordered 100 units but supplier can only deliver the 60 already received. Short-close the rest.
Yes, close the remaining 40.
check: Journey locked = PoScl
Slots extracted: po_number=PO-2026-805, line_no=3, ordered_qty=100, received_qty=60
short_close_reason captured (free-text)
Final commit = POSCLMAINSBT

title: Mid-flow change of mind (splice retraction)
context: Test that the bot can retract a splice cleanly when the user changes their mind. Started as Capital PO, switches to standard.
turns: Create a Capital PO for SUP-CAP-2.
Actually, this is just a standard expense, not Capital. Never mind.
USD, today, buyer BUY-9, numbering PO/STD/2026. Line: ITM-1, 50 units at 10.
Create it.
check: Turn 1: splice_triggered = po_type==Capital
Turn 2: splice retraction — splice_triggered becomes None, capital_proposal_id no longer required
supplier_code (SUP-CAP-2) is PRESERVED from turn 1 — bot doesn't lose context
Final commit fires successfully without capital_proposal_id

title: Cross-module: PR to PO conversion
covers: PoCrt + cross-module PR reference + PR Coverage splice
context: Test cross-module awareness. User wants to create a PO that covers a Purchase Request. Bot should recognize PR-2026-220 references the PR module and walk the PR-Coverage splice.
turns: I have approved purchase request PR-2026-220. Create a direct PO for SUP-101 to cover it.
Use the PR's line items as-is. USD, today, buyer BUY-60. Numbering PO/STD/2026.
Yes, all PO lines cover the PR 1-to-1.
Create the PO.
check: Journey locked = PoCrt (NOT a PR journey — user explicitly said 'direct PO')
PR Coverage splice opens (ui_splice_requested=pr_cov or splice_walked includes pr_coverage_via_LNK9)
pr_coverage_ref or pr_number slot captured = PR-2026-220
Final commit = POCRTMAINSBT
Files-referenced popup should mention this is a cross-module touch
